data.title: French and Benelux stocks-Factors to watch
data.description:
Below are company-related news and stories from France and Benelux which could have an impact on the region's markets or individual stocks.

Air France-KLM

French-Dutch airline group Air France-KLM issued a 500-million-euro note under its EMTN programme, with a five-year maturity, a 3.75% coupon, and a 3.866% yield.

Carrefour

French retailer Carrefour placed a 500-million-euro three-year bond, settling and maturing in December 2028, with a 2.875% annual coupon.

Orange

French telecom Orange issued 900 million euros in new bonds, including a 12-year tranche maturing in September 2037 with a 3.75% coupon.

Schneider Electric

Electrical equipment maker Schneider Electric issued 3.5 billion euros in EMTN bonds across four tranches: a variable-coupon tranche maturing in September 2027, a four-year tranche with a 2.625% fixed coupon, a 6.5-year tranche with a 3.0% fixed rate, and a 12-year tranche with a 3.624% fixed coupon.

Vinci

French concessions and construction company Vinci reported a 1.0% rise in motorway traffic in July 2025, while airport passenger traffic increased by 3.6% over the same period.

data.title: Indonesia Stocks Fall from Record High Amid Ongoing Protests
data.description:
The IDX Composite dropped 163 points, or 2.3%, to 7,770 around noon on Friday, halting gains from the previous two sessions and marking its lowest level in over two weeks.

The decline came as traders engaged in profit-taking after the broader index hit a record high the previous day.

Sentiment was also weighed down by rising political tensions, with an increasing number of demonstrations questioning the performance of parliament on Thursday and continuing into today.

Ongoing foreign outflows added to the downward pressure.

However, an upbeat session on Wall Street overnight helped cap the decline.

Traders are also awaiting the release of key domestic economic indicators, including August consumer prices and July trade data, next Monday.

All sectors were in the red, led by primary consumer, property, and raw materials.

Major laggards included Bank Jago (-8.1%), Teknologi Karya Digital (-7.1%), Hetzer Medical Indonesia (-5.4%), and Amman Mineral Internasional (-4.9%)

data.title: Cambricon Technologies Warns of Trading Risks After Stock Surges — Update
data.description:
By Sherry Qin

Chinese artificial-intelligence chip maker Cambricon Technologies issued a warning about trading risks after a surge in its stock price over the past month.

The company's stock gains have exceeded that of most of its industry peers and major indexes, the Beijing-based company said in an exchange filing late Thursday.

There is a risk that the stock price may have deviated from current fundamentals, as its current price-to-earnings ratio is over 5000 times, far exceeding the industry level, the company said.

Cambricon's Shanghai-listed stock fell 5.6% as of midday on Friday after the warning. Despite the decline, its shares have more than doubled over the past month and have overtaken liquor maker Kweichow Moutai as China's most valuable stock.

Cambricon's rise comes amid a broader market rally in China, with the benchmark Shanghai Composite Index gaining over 6% over the past month and notching a 10-year high earlier this month.

China's AI push and drive to make chips locally have given the AI chip designer another push as domestic investors shift their focus to AI infrastructure names.

Another catalyst behind the stock's gains was Goldman Sachs raising its target price on Cambricon by 50% over the weekend to 1,835.00 yuan.

Cambricon also clarified Thursday that it doesn't have plans to launch new products.

Chinese demand for AI inferencing has been rising after the launch of local foundation models such as DeepSeek, while domestic clients are diversifying their channels for obtaining chips amid tariff uncertainty and data-security concerns.

DeepSeek said last week that its new model uses a new format designed for next-generation homegrown chips, indicating a potential breakthrough in Chinese AI hardware. Markets have speculated that the latest AI chip could come from Cambricon.

Cambricon expects its 2025 revenue to be between 5 billion yuan, equivalent to $701.2 million, and 7 billion yuan, after its first-half revenue grew roughly 44-fold to 2.88 billion yuan.

Some analysts are cautious about the frenzy over Cambricon and China's ambition to produce AI chips at home.

Cambricon, as a fabless chip maker, can't turn into TSMC, the world's largest contract chip maker overnight, said Morningstar analyst Phelix Lee. Meanwhile, SMIC, China's largest chip maker, still has very low yields in manufacturing advanced chips, Lee added.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com

data.title: Yuan hits fresh 10-month high on firm PBOC fixing, stock market rally
data.description:
China's yuan rallied to a 10-month high against the dollar on Friday, poised for its biggest monthly gain since May, underpinned by firm central bank fixings and a buoyant stock market.

The onshore yuan strengthened to 7.126 per dollar, the strongest since November 6, 2024, before paring some gains to trade largely flat by 0415 GMT.

Its offshore counterpart was little changed at 7.124, after briefly breaching the 7.12 level to hit a 10-month high of 7.1160 overnight.

Recent gains in China's stock market and expectations the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates next month should help restore investor confidence and support the yuan, said Christopher Wong, FX strategist at OCBC Bank.

"There could be further room for RMB to appreciate should China's economy see more sustained stabilization."

The dollar's six-currency index headed for a monthly fall of 2% on ramped-up bets of an imminent Fed easing.

The yuan is up 1.0% against the dollar this month, and is on track for its biggest gain since May as the People's Bank of China continued to signal support for a stronger currency with firm daily fixings.

The offshore yuan has also risen 1.2% this month, marking its best performance in a year.

Prior to the market opening, the central bank set the midpoint rate at 7.1030 per dollar, 244 pips firmer than a Reuters' estimate and the strongest level since November 6. The spot yuan is allowed to trade a maximum of 2% either side of the fixed midpoint each day.

"There has been asymmetric bias for USD/CNY fixing to go lower. This shows PBoC's intention to engineer gradual RMB appreciation post U.S.-China trade truce mid-May," Goldman Sachs said in a note, projecting USD/CNH to grind lower to 7.1 in the next 1-2 months and 7.0 by year-end.

Elsewhere, the Hong Kong Interbank Offered Rate (HIBOR) (HIBOR=) continued to rise across the board, reflecting recent cash withdrawals by the city's de facto central bank and sharp rebounds in the local dollar.

The overnight tenor (HIHKDOND=) climbed above 4% on Friday for the first time since May, marking the largest monthly rebound since records began in 2006.

Key onshore vs offshore levels:

* Overnight dollar/yuan swap onshore -21.00 pips vs. offshore -21.00

* Three-month SHIBOR (SHIBOR=) 1.6 % vs. 3-month CNH HIBOR 1.7 %

data.title: Sun TV Network share price falls 2.05% in early trade
data.description:
Shares of Sun TV Network declined 2.05% to Rs 541.30 in early trade. The stock is part of the Nifty Midcap 150 index.

The company's consolidated revenue for the quarter-ending June 2025 stood at Rs 1,290.28 Crore, compared to Rs 1,312.40 Crore in June 2024. The net profit for the quarter-ending June 2025 was Rs 531.94 Crore, while in June 2024, it was Rs 557.19 Crore. The EPS for June 2025 was Rs 13.43, compared to Rs 14.20 in June 2024.

The consolidated revenue for the year-ending March 2025 was Rs 4,015.09 Crore, compared to Rs 4,282.10 Crore in March 2024. The net profit for the year-ending March 2025 was Rs 1,708.54 Crore, down from Rs 1,915.53 Crore in March 2024. The EPS decreased to Rs 43.23 in March 2025 from Rs 48.86 in March 2024.

data.title: Turkey - Factors to watch on Aug 29
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Friday.

The lira was quoted at 41.15 against the U.S. dollar at 0437 GMT, compared with a close of 40.02 on Thursday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 0.09% higher at 11,368.78 points on Thursday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asian stocks edged higher on Friday, riding a tech-driven rally on Wall Street, with investor focus now turning to a key U.S. inflation reading due later in the day for further clues on the Federal Reserve's rate outlook.

PARLIAMENT MEETING ON GAZA

The Turkish Parliament's general assembly will convene for an extraordinary meeting chaired by Speaker Numan Kurtulmus over Israel's attacks in Gaza. (1100 GMT)

VICE PRESIDENT

Turkish Vice President Cevdet Yilmaz will chair a meeting of the Economic Coordination Council (EKK). (0600 GMT)

ENVIRONMENT MINISTER

Environment, Urbanisation and Climate Minister Murat Kurum will attend a foundation-laying ceremony for new housing units to be built in the western province of Izmir's Odemis district, which was hit by a wildfire. (1130 GMT)

INDUSTRY MINISTER

Industry and Technology Minister Mehmet Fatih Kacir will attend the opening of TUBITAK's Sky Observation event in the northeastern province of Erzurum. (1630 GMT)

UNEMPLOYMENT DATA (TRUR=ECI)

The Turkish Statistical Institute will release the July labour force statistics. (0700 GMT)

BANKING SECTOR DATA

The BDDK banking watchdog will release banking sector data for July. (1100 GMT)

UKRAINE PEACE EFFORTS

Turkish Foreign Minister Hakan Fidan and U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio discussed peace efforts on the Russia-Ukraine conflict in a phone call on Thursday, a Turkish Foreign Ministry source said.

President Volodymyr Zelenskiy expects a framework for security guarantees for Ukraine to be set out as soon as next week, as Kyiv continues talks with allies to get a robust support mechanism to prevent further Russian aggression.

PKK DISARMAMENT

Senior Kurdish lawmakers in Turkey say a lasting peace with the Kurdistan Workers Party (PKK) requires legal protections for militants who disarm, direct talks with the group's jailed leader and an easing of a crackdown on the opposition.

MILITARY ESPIONAGE INVESTIGATION

Turkish authorities have detained the owner and general manager of Assan Group, a defence and construction conglomerate, as part of a military espionage investigation, the Istanbul prosecutor's office said late on Wednesday.

TURK TELEKOM

Turk Telekom's shares dropped more than 5% on Wednesday after the company promised to spend billions of dollars upgrading Turkey's fixed-line telecoms in return for an extension of its license to operate the network until 2050.

INDIGO-TURKISH AIRLINES

India will allow IndiGo to continue leasing two planes from Turkish Airlines for another six months, India's largest airline said on Thursday, in a reversal of a decision from May when New Delhi told IndiGo to end the arrangement by August 31.

data.title: Hindustan Unilever shares rise 2.10% in early trade, among top gainers on Nifty 50
data.description:
Shares of Hindustan Unilever gained 2.10% to trade at Rs 2,710.10 in Friday's early session, as of 9.30 am. The stock is among the top gainers on the Nifty 50 index. This represents a Rs 55.70 increase compared to its previous close.

Hindustan Unilever's revenue for the quarter-ending June 2025 stood at Rs 16,514 Crore, up from Rs 15,670 Crore in March 2025. The net profit for the same period was Rs 2,769 Crore, compared to Rs 2,476 Crore in the previous quarter. The EPS for June 2025 was Rs 11.73, up from Rs 10.48 in March 2025. For the quarter ending June 2024, the company reported revenue of Rs 15,707 Crore and net profit of Rs 2,614 Crore.

The company's annual financial performance shows consistent growth. Revenue for the year-ending March 2025 reached Rs 63,121 Crore, compared to Rs 61,896 Crore in March 2024. Net profit increased to Rs 10,679 Crore from Rs 10,286 Crore during the same period. The EPS also saw an increase, reaching Rs 45.32, up from Rs 43.74 in the previous year.

The company announced a final dividend of Rs 24 per share on April 24, 2025, with an effective date of June 23, 2025. Additionally, an interim dividend of Rs 19 per share and a special dividend of Rs 10 per share were announced on October 10 and October 11, 2024, respectively, both effective November 6, 2024. A stock split was announced on July 5, 2000, with an ex-split date of July 3, 2000, changing the face value from Rs 10 to Rs 1.

Moneycontrol analysis indicates very bullish sentiment for Hindustan Unilever as of today.With the stock's last traded price at Rs 2,710.10, Hindustan Unilever has seen a significant rise from its previous close, driven by positive financial results and dividend announcements.

data.title: CIMB: 2Q Net Profit Fell on Lower Interest Income
Rupiah Slides to 4-Week Low on Lawmaker Perks Backlash
data.description:
The Indonesian rupiah slipped to around 16,430 per USD, its weakest level since early August, amid mounting public anger over lawmakers’ new perks.

At the center of the controversy is a housing allowance of IDR 50 million per month for members of parliament, roughly five times Jakarta’s minimum wage.

The backlash has intensified amid rising property taxes imposed by regional governments, following President Prabowo Subianto’s draft 2026 budget that shifts more fiscal responsibility to local authorities while reducing central funding.

Local media reported that police fired tear gas and used water cannons to disperse protesters, underscoring growing discontent.

The unrest comes as households already face high food prices and sluggish job growth.

Meanwhile, Bank Indonesia pledged to stay active in the FX market, intervening in spot, forward, and bond markets to stabilize the rupiah.

Meanwhile, the US dollar steadied as investors awaited the PCE inflation report for clues on Fed policy.

data.title: Cambricon Technologies Warns of Trading Risks After Stock Surges — Update
data.description:
By Sherry Qin

Chinese artificial-intelligence chip maker Cambricon Technologies issued a warning about trading risks after a surge in its stock price over the past month.

The company's stock gains have exceeded that of most of its industry peers and major indexes, the Beijing-based company said in an exchange filing late Thursday.

There is a risk that the stock price may have deviated from current fundamentals, as its current price-to-earnings ratio is over 5000 times, far exceeding the industry level, the company said.

Cambricon's Shanghai-listed stock fell 5.6% as of midday on Friday after the warning. Despite the decline, its shares have more than doubled over the past month and have overtaken liquor maker Kweichow Moutai as China's most valuable stock.

Cambricon's rise comes amid a broader market rally in China, with the benchmark Shanghai Composite Index gaining over 6% over the past month and notching a 10-year high earlier this month.

China's AI push and drive to make chips locally have given the AI chip designer another push as domestic investors shift their focus to AI infrastructure names.

Another catalyst behind the stock's gains was Goldman Sachs raising its target price on Cambricon by 50% over the weekend to 1,835.00 yuan.

Cambricon also clarified Thursday that it doesn't have plans to launch new products.

Chinese demand for AI inferencing has been rising after the launch of local foundation models such as DeepSeek, while domestic clients are diversifying their channels for obtaining chips amid tariff uncertainty and data-security concerns.

DeepSeek said last week that its new model uses a new format designed for next-generation homegrown chips, indicating a potential breakthrough in Chinese AI hardware. Markets have speculated that the latest AI chip could come from Cambricon.

Cambricon expects its 2025 revenue to be between 5 billion yuan, equivalent to $701.2 million, and 7 billion yuan, after its first-half revenue grew roughly 44-fold to 2.88 billion yuan.

Some analysts are cautious about the frenzy over Cambricon and China's ambition to produce AI chips at home.

Cambricon, as a fabless chip maker, can't turn into TSMC, the world's largest contract chip maker overnight, said Morningstar analyst Phelix Lee. Meanwhile, SMIC, China's largest chip maker, still has very low yields in manufacturing advanced chips, Lee added.

Write to Sherry Qin at sherry.qin@wsj.com

data.title: India bonds dip ahead of fresh debt supply
data.description:
Indian government bonds drifted lower in early trading on Friday as traders pared positions ahead of the weekly debt sale, while keeping an eye on the economic growth data due later in the day.

The benchmark 10-year bond yield (IN063335G=CC) was at 6.5578% as of 10:10 a.m. IST, after closing at 6.5328% on Thursday.

New Delhi is set to sell bonds worth 320 billion Indian rupees ($3.65 billion), with maturities of 15 years and 40 years.

"We are seeing some selloff as traders are bracing for the auction, but the cut-offs will eventually decide the direction of the yields," a trader at a private bank said.

The 10-year bond yield dropped nearly 7 basis points on Thursday, on short covering due to expectations of a central bank intervention to support bonds.

Investors will also closely watch for gross domestic product data, due later in the day.

India’s economy likely slowed in the April-June quarter to 6.7% year-on-year, from 7.4% in the previous three months, according to a Reuters poll of economists.

The benchmark 10-year bond yield is set to log its biggest monthly jump in three years if the yield sustains above 6.53%.

Bond yields have sharply risen this month, after a hawkish central bank monetary policy and the government proposed sweeping tax cuts, fueling fears of fiscal slippage and a heavier debt supply.

Bond traders are now calling for the Reserve Bank of India's intervention as a sharp drop in institutional buying has pushed yields higher, threatening to stall monetary transmission, according to market participants.

RATES

India's overnight index swap rates are expected to be little changed after seeing strong receiving in the previous session.

The one-year OIS rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) and the two-year OIS OIS rate (INR2YMIBROIS=CC) were not yet traded. The liquid five-year OIS rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) stood steady at 5.74%.

($1 = 87.77 Indian rupees)

data.title: Musk seeks dismissal of SEC lawsuit on his 2022 Twitter stake
data.description:
Billionaire Elon Musk filed a motion on Thursday to dismiss the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission's (SEC) civil lawsuit that accused him of waiting too long in 2022 to reveal his large stake in social media platform Twitter, later renamed as X.

In a complaint filed in Washington, D.C. federal court in January, the SEC said Musk violated federal securities law by waiting 11 days too long to disclose his initial purchase of 5% of Twitter's common shares. It sought to force Musk to pay a civil fine and give up profits that the SEC said were a result of the violations.

Lawyers for Musk said on Thursday the billionaire stopped purchasing additional shares of then-publicly-listed Twitter and filed his disclosure one business day after his wealth manager consulted securities disclosure counsel about potential filing requirements.

An SEC rule requires investors to disclose within 10 calendar days when they cross a 5% ownership threshold, which would have been by March 24, 2022 in Musk's case.

The SEC said that at the expense of unsuspecting investors, Musk instead bought more than $500 million of Twitter shares at artificially low prices before finally revealing his purchases on April 4, 2022, by which time he owned a 9.2% stake.

The SEC sued Musk on January 14, six days before Republican President Donald Trump took office and made Musk a special adviser to slash the federal workforce and spending.

Musk faced a deadline to respond to the court by Friday.

Musk's lawyers said the case should have not been brought and the billionaire did not mean any harm. They said the SEC's action against Musk "reveals an agency targeting an individual for his protected criticism of government overreach."

"The SEC does not allege that Mr. Musk acted intentionally, deliberately, willfully, or even recklessly... Rather, the SEC alleges that Mr. Musk late-filed a single beneficial ownership form three years ago, and fully corrected any alleged error immediately upon its discovery. There is no ongoing violation," the Tesla and SpaceX CEO's lawyers said.

The SEC did not respond to a request for comment outside regular business hours.

Musk has long feuded with the SEC, including after it sued him in 2018 over his Twitter posts about possibly taking Tesla private and having secured funding to do so.

data.title: Australia's Immuron rises as full-year loss shrinks, revenue grows
data.description:
** Shares of Immuron rise as much as 4.4% to A$0.072, their highest level since August 20

** The biopharmaceutical co's annual loss after income tax expense attributable shrinks to A$5.3 million ($3.38 million) from a loss of A$6.9 million last year

** Reports FY revenue of A$7.3 million from A$4.9 million a year ago

** Stock down 8.9% YTD, including the session's moves

($1 = 1.5389 Australian dollars)
